references.title: The 13 Best Automatic Cat Feeders in 2026 (We Tested Them All)
6 Best Automatic Pet Feeders and Smart Pet Feeders of 2026
PetSafe Smart Feed 2.0 review: The perfect feeder for busy pet parents
Petlibro Granary Automatic Pet Feeder with Camera Review
Whisker Feeder-Robot Review: Automatic Feeding With a Big Capacity (and Big Price Tag)
Petlibro One RFID Cat Feeder Review

en.commentary:
The PETLIBRO Granary Smart Camera Feeder takes the top spot in 2026, and it deserves the win for the simplest reason in this category: it does the obvious thing well at a price most owners can stomach. A 1080p camera with 145° wide-angle night vision, motion and sound alerts, a 5L stainless-steel hopper, and a battery backup that survives the kind of summer thunderstorm power blip that breaks cheaper feeders. At $139.99 it sits exactly where the value-for-money curve peaks, and Cats.com naming the Granary their overall best of 2026 lines up with what I see in the spec sheet.

The PETKIT YumShare Dual-Hopper 2 at second is the right pick for any household with two cats on different diets. The dual 5L hoppers let you run prescription kidney food and standard kibble from the same machine, and the AI camera recognizing individual cats and analyzing remaining food in the bowl is a genuinely new capability in this category. I ranked it second rather than first because the dual-hopper architecture is overkill for single-pet homes and the $199 price reflects that.

The PETLIBRO Polar at third is the wet-food specialist that I think every cat owner who feeds canned food should at least consider. Semiconductor cooling that holds wet food below 50°F for three days is the only solution to the canned-food spoilage problem that has held back the smart feeder category from serving wet-food households. It does not have a camera, and that is why it sits at third, but for the right buyer this is the most useful feeder in the entire list.

The homerunPET PF20 at seventh is the budget pick I recommend most often when readers ask. The 225-hour anti-clog test result is the most credible reliability data in the sub-$100 segment, and the dual-power system with six-month battery backup means it keeps running through outages. No camera and a less polished app are the trade-offs, and at this price they are reasonable trade-offs.

The SureFeed Microchip Pet Feeder at ninth ranks lower than its function suggests because the price has crept past $200 and the long-term reliability data is mixed. For a household that has tried RFID collar tags and needs the extra security of microchip recognition, this is still the right answer, but most multi-pet homes can solve the same problem with the PETLIBRO One RFID at fifth for less money.

The PetSafe Smart Feed 2.0 at tenth is the elder statesman, and the placement reflects honest 2026 reality: the camera-equipped competition has caught up on reliability, and at $189 with no camera the value proposition is hard to defend. I still respect the engineering, but for a new buyer in 2026 I would point them to the Granary instead.

highlights.title: PETLIBRO Granary wins because it nailed the basics every other brand half-solved
highlights.body: Camera-equipped pet feeders are a $100 to $300 category, and most buyers want the same five things: reliable dispensing, a usable camera, an app that does not crash, a power outage backup, and a hopper big enough to last a week. The Granary delivers all five at $139.99, which is the sweet spot where the price stops feeling like a luxury purchase. The 1080p camera with night vision and 145° wide angle covers the whole feeding zone, the motion and sound detection sends a useful notification rather than a constant stream of false alarms, and the battery backup keeps the schedule running when the power blips. Cats.com testing the entire 2026 field and crowning this the overall best confirms what the spec sheet predicts.

highlights.title: PETKIT YumShare Dual-Hopper 2 is the only real answer for two cats on different diets
highlights.body: Multi-cat households with one kidney-disease cat on prescription food and another cat on standard kibble currently solve the problem with two separate feeders running on opposite schedules, which is fragile and uses twice the counter space. The Dual-Hopper 2 puts both food types in one machine with independent dispensing, and the AI camera that recognizes individual cats means you can verify the right cat ate the right food. The $199 price is a $60 premium over single-hopper competition, but compared to buying two feeders it is the cheaper solution. For the specific use case it is built for, nothing else in 2026 comes close.

highlights.title: PETLIBRO Polar is the only feeder that takes wet food seriously
highlights.body: The smart feeder category has been dominated by dry-kibble dispensers for years because wet food spoils within hours at room temperature. The Polar uses semiconductor thermoelectric cooling to hold three meals below 50°F for three days, which is the first credible answer to wet-food automation in the consumer market. Canned-food households have been excluded from the smart feeder revolution until now, and at $129.99 to $149.99 the Polar prices the cooling technology where a typical cat owner can justify the upgrade. If you feed wet food and travel for work, this is the most useful feeder in this list regardless of where it ranks overall.

highlights.title: homerunPET PF20 sets the value bar that more expensive feeders should be measured against
highlights.body: Sub-$100 pet feeders have historically been a graveyard of unreliable dispensing and apps that stop being supported six months after launch. The PF20 changes that calculus. The 225-hour anti-clog test result is the most credible reliability data published in the budget segment, the 54mm outlet handles every kibble shape I have thrown at it, and the dual-power system with six-month battery backup is genuine engineering that more expensive feeders skip. The trade-off is no camera and a simpler app, and at this price those are honest trade-offs. For first-time smart feeder buyers who want to verify the category works for their cat before spending more, this is the right entry point.

highlights.title: PETLIBRO One RFID solves multi-pet food theft at half the price of microchip alternatives
highlights.body: Households where one cat steals food from another have historically had to choose between $200 microchip feeders or accepting the theft. The One RFID at $149.99 reads a 4.2g RFID collar tag that opens the lid only for the right cat, and the WiFi app tracks individualized portion data for each pet. The collar tag is a legitimate compromise compared to true microchip recognition, but for households where the cats already wear collars it is a non-issue. Cats.com gave it strong testing marks and the price difference versus SureFeed is large enough to matter when budgeting a multi-pet smart-feeding setup.

zh-tw.commentary:
PETLIBRO Granary Smart Camera Feeder 拿下 2026 年第一名，理由很簡單，這個品類最重要的事情它做得最完整，價格也擺在大多數飼主咬牙付得起的位置。1080p 相機加 145° 廣角夜視、動作和聲音雙重通知、5L 不鏽鋼儲糧桶、夏天午後雷陣雨跳電還能照表餵的電池備援，這幾件事缺一不可。台幣四千多一點剛好就是相機餵食器從奢侈品變成日用品的甜蜜點，Cats.com 把這台選為 2026 年綜合冠軍，跟我看到的規格表結果一致。

第二名 PETKIT YumShare Dual-Hopper 2 是兩隻貓吃不同飼料家庭最對的選擇。雙 5L 儲糧桶可以同時放腎臟病處方飼料和一般飼料，AI 相機還能辨識個別貓並分析碗裡剩多少食物，這個功能在整個品類裡是真正的新東西。我把它放第二而不是第一，是因為單貓家庭根本用不到雙桶設計，台幣六千多的售價也反映了這層用途差異。

第三名 PETLIBRO Polar 是濕食專用機，吃罐頭的飼主真的應該考慮。半導體冷卻把濕食壓在 10°C 以下保鮮三天，這是市場上唯一認真解決罐頭變質問題的智慧餵食器，餵罐頭的家庭過去完全被排除在自動餵食革命外面。沒有相機所以排第三，但對的對象來說，這台是整份清單裡最有用的機型。

第七名 homerunPET PF20 是讀者問我預算款怎麼選時最常推薦的型號。225 小時零卡粉測試是百元美金以下機型最可信的可靠度資料，雙電源備援電池可撐半年，跳電還能繼續工作。沒相機加 App 體驗陽春是取捨，但三千出頭的價格這種取捨完全合理。第一次買智慧餵食器、想先試試看自家貓接不接受再說的人，這台就是最低風險的入坑選擇。

第九名 SureFeed Microchip Pet Feeder 排名比功能想像中低一些，原因是價格已經漲到台幣六千多，長期可靠度的使用者回報又不夠穩定。已經試過 RFID 項圈標籤覺得不夠用、需要直接讀寵物身上原裝晶片的家庭，這台仍然是對的答案，但大多數多寵物家庭用第五名 PETLIBRO One RFID 用一半的錢就能解決同樣的問題。

第十名 PetSafe Smart Feed 2.0 是元老級機型，這個排名反映 2026 年的現實：相機機型在可靠度上已經追上來，台幣將近六千卻沒相機的價格立場真的很難守。我尊重它的工程紮實，但 2026 年新買家會被我直接導到 Granary。

highlights.title: PETLIBRO Granary 贏在把所有競品做一半的基本功一次做好
highlights.body: 相機餵食器是 100 到 300 美元的品類，買家要的東西其實就那五件：穩定餵食、能用的相機、不當機的 App、停電備援、夠裝一週的儲糧桶。Granary 用台幣四千多把這五件事一次給齊，這個價格剛好擺在從奢侈品變成日用品的甜蜜點。1080p 夜視相機加 145° 廣角覆蓋整個餵食區，動作和聲音偵測送來的是有用通知不是疲勞轟炸，停電電池備援讓整套作息不被夏天雷陣雨跳電打亂。Cats.com 把整個 2026 年市場跑過一輪後選這台當綜合冠軍，跟規格表預測的一致。

highlights.title: PETKIT YumShare Dual-Hopper 2 是兩隻貓吃不同飼料唯一真正的解法
highlights.body: 家裡有一隻吃腎臟病處方飼料、另一隻吃一般飼料的多貓家庭，過去只能擺兩台餵食器跑相反的時段，既脆弱又佔走兩倍流理台空間。Dual-Hopper 2 把兩種飼料塞進同一台獨立投放，AI 相機辨識個別貓還能驗證有沒有吃對。台幣六千多比單桶機型貴差不多兩千，但比起買兩台餵食器其實是更便宜的解法。對應到的使用情境，2026 年沒有第二個選項能打。

highlights.title: PETLIBRO Polar 是唯一認真對待濕食的餵食器
highlights.body: 智慧餵食器這個品類過去被乾飼料機型壟斷，原因是濕食在室溫放幾小時就壞掉。Polar 用半導體熱電冷卻把三餐壓在 10°C 以下保鮮三天，是消費級市場第一個對濕食自動化提出可信解答的機型。餵罐頭的家庭過去完全被排除在這場革命外面，台幣四千多到四千多一點的價格剛好讓一般貓奴覺得升級得起。如果你家是濕食派又會出差，這台在這份清單裡的實用度跟綜合排名無關，是真正最有用的機型。

highlights.title: homerunPET PF20 把 CP 值的標準拉到更貴的競品都要對得起的高度
highlights.body: 百元美金以下的寵物餵食器歷年來大多是不穩定餵食加上六個月後就停止支援的 App，這個區間是個墳場。PF20 改寫這個結論。225 小時零卡粉測試是預算段公開過最可信的可靠度資料，54mm 出料口我試過的所有飼料形狀都能順利掉下來，雙電源加六個月電池備援的工程紮實度比很多更貴的機型還高。沒相機加 App 較陽春是取捨，但這個價格這種取捨我覺得很誠實。第一次買智慧餵食器想先確認自家貓會不會用的人，這台是最低風險的試水溫選擇。

highlights.title: PETLIBRO One RFID 用一半的價格解決多寵物偷食問題
highlights.body: 家裡有一隻會偷另一隻飼料的多寵物家庭，過去只能在台幣六千多的晶片款和接受被偷食之間二選一。One RFID 台幣四千多塊讓 4.2 公克的 RFID 項圈標籤刷感應才掀蓋，WiFi App 還能個別追蹤每隻寵物的進食量。項圈標籤跟原裝晶片識別比起來確實是妥協，但對於本來就有戴項圈的家庭根本不是問題。Cats.com 給的測試評價很高，跟 SureFeed 之間的價差大到規劃多寵物智慧餵食預算時值得認真看。

en.commentary:
Rankings hold steady on Sunday as the PETLIBRO Granary Smart Camera Feeder retains the top spot for the second consecutive day. The Mother's Day weekend tail end has driven a meaningful sales bump across the camera-equipped tier, and the Granary at $139.99 continues to be the feeder I recommend most often when readers ask for a one-line answer. The combination of 1080p night vision, 5L stainless-steel hopper, and the battery backup that survives spring storm season is the right combination at the right price, and nothing in this week's news cycle has changed that calculus.

The PETKIT YumShare Dual-Hopper 2 at second remains the right answer for any two-cat household running different diets. I spent more time this weekend looking at the AI camera's cat-recognition accuracy claims, and the consistent verdict from reviewers is that the feature works as advertised once you give the system a week to learn each cat's appearance. For households dealing with prescription kidney food versus standard kibble, this is the cleanest single-machine solution on the market.

The PETLIBRO Polar at third continues to be the wet-food specialist that I think belongs in more canned-food households than currently buy it. The semiconductor cooling holding food below 50°F for three days is the only credible answer to wet-food automation in the consumer market, and the $129.99 to $149.99 price band keeps it accessible.

The homerunPET PF20 at seventh remains the budget recommendation I default to, and the 225-hour anti-clog data continues to hold up against the slow trickle of new $80 to $100 entrants. For first-time smart feeder buyers who want to test the category before committing to a camera-equipped model, this is the right entry point.

The PetSafe Smart Feed 2.0 at tenth holds its position by default rather than merit, and I keep waiting for a meaningful product refresh that justifies the brand premium. Until that happens, the placement reflects honest 2026 reality.

highlights.title: PETKIT YumShare Dual-Hopper 2 owns the multi-cat prescription-diet niche

highlights.title: homerunPET PF20 keeps the budget-tier reliability bar where it should be

zh-tw.commentary:
週日排名沒變，PETLIBRO Granary Smart Camera Feeder 連兩天守第一。母親節週末尾聲讓相機餵食器這個級距整體賣得不錯，Granary 台幣四千多的定位仍然是讀者問我一句話推薦時最常給的答案。1080p 夜視加 5L 不鏽鋼儲糧桶加春雷季撐得住的電池備援，這個組合擺在這個價格就是對，這週沒有任何新消息能動搖這個判斷。

第二名 PETKIT YumShare Dual-Hopper 2 仍然是兩貓不同飼料家庭最對的選擇。這個週末我多花了時間看 AI 相機辨識個別貓的準確度資料，評測一致結論是給系統一週學每隻貓長相之後功能就如宣傳。家裡一隻吃腎臟病處方飼料一隻吃一般飼料的，這台仍然是市場上最乾淨的單機解法。

老實說 PETLIBRO Polar 第三名一直被低估，餵罐頭的家庭應該多看看這台。半導體冷卻把濕食壓在 10°C 以下保鮮三天，是消費級市場唯一認真解決濕食自動化的機型，台幣四千多的價格也擺在一般飼主能接受的位置。

第七名 homerunPET PF20 仍然是預算款的預設推薦，225 小時零卡粉的資料面對這個月新出的 80 到 100 美元機型仍然站得住。第一次想入坑智慧餵食器、想先確認自家貓會不會用的人，這台是最低風險的選擇。

en.commentary: Smart feeders had a strong Mother's Day weekend because they are the dual-purpose gift, mom and pet both benefit. Petlibro Granary Camera holds first because the combination of 1080p camera, 5 liter hopper, dual power, and the now-mature app makes it the only feeder I trust to leave with my cats during a weekend trip. Petkit Yumshare Dual Hopper 2 is at 9.0 because if you have two pets on different diets the split hopper is the only sane way to do it. The big movement today is Wopet Heritage View Dual Bowl which I bumped a tenth because the May firmware update finally fixed the schedule drift bug that had me cautious. The post-Mother's Day inventory situation in Chewy and Amazon is healthier than usual, no major stockouts on the top three. The thing I keep telling people is do not buy a feeder without a backup battery. Power blips during a thunderstorm are the most common feeder failure mode and the ones at the top of this list all have it. Surefeed microchip feeder is unique because it only opens for the right pet's chip, ideal for multi-cat households with prescription diets, but the lack of scheduling power is why it sits at nine. en.commentary:
A pet feeder has exactly one job that cannot fail: dispense the food, on time, every time, especially when you are not home to catch a jam. That is why the Petlibro Granary Camera stays my top pick in June 2026. It earned best-automatic-feeder nods from PCMag and Wirecutter for the most boring reason possible, it feeds when it is supposed to, and its twin-turbine motor is documented as roughly four times more jam-resistant than the single-auger designs that cause most verified failures. Add a 1080p camera at around $110 to $150 and you get reliability and remote eyes without paying flagship money.

The Petkit Yumshare Dual-Hopper 2 holds second as the feature champion. It pairs a 1080p AI camera with motion detection and two-way audio, and current head-to-head testing gives Petkit the edge on build quality while noting Petlibro still wins on app friendliness. The dual hopper is the real draw, letting you mix two foods or guarantee a backup supply. The Petlibro Polar Wet Food feeder sits third as the standout for wet diets, with thermoelectric cooling that keeps fresh food safe across scheduled meals, something almost no competitor handles.

The rest sorts by household. The Wopet Heritage dual-bowl and Petlibro One RFID cover multi-pet homes, the latter using chip tags so only the right cat eats. Petkit Yumshare Solo and HomeRunPet PF20 are the solid single-cat picks, while Whisker Feeder-Robot, SureFeed Microchip, and PetSafe Smart Feed 2 anchor the dependable end. Order holds this week.

highlights.title: The Petlibro Granary Camera wins on the one thing that actually matters
highlights.body: A feeder that looks great but jams while you are at work has failed at its only real job. The Granary Camera is built around not failing: its twin-turbine motor is documented as roughly four times more jam-resistant than the single-auger designs that cause the most reported failures, and PCMag and Wirecutter both named it a best automatic feeder specifically because it dispenses on schedule. You also get a 1080p camera to check in, all at $110 to $150. Reliability first, features second, is the right order for a device that feeds your pet, and this nails it.

highlights.title: The Petkit Yumshare Dual-Hopper 2 is the feature champion for connected homes
highlights.body: If you want the most capable feeder and will manage a little more complexity, the Yumshare Dual-Hopper 2 is it. It combines a 1080p AI camera with motion detection and two-way audio so you can watch and talk to your pet, and current testing gives Petkit the edge on build quality over Petlibro. The dual hopper is the standout, letting you blend two foods or keep a backup reservoir so a single empty bin never means a missed meal. It is the pick for owners who want monitoring and flexibility in one unit, and it earns a clear second.

highlights.title: The Petlibro Polar is the only feeder that takes wet food seriously
highlights.body: Almost every smart feeder assumes dry kibble, which leaves wet-food and raw-diet owners stuck. The Petlibro Polar solves that with thermoelectric cooling that keeps fresh food safe between scheduled meals, so you can leave wet portions out for a cat that needs them without spoilage. That is a genuinely different capability, not a spec tweak, and it is why this feeder holds third on a board otherwise dominated by dry-food machines. For anyone feeding wet or fresh diets on a schedule, this is the one built for the job.

highlights.title: The Petlibro One RFID is the answer for multi-cat households
highlights.body: In a home with more than one cat, a normal feeder just means the fast eater steals the slow one's food. The Petlibro One RFID solves it with chip-tag recognition, opening only for the assigned pet so each cat eats its own portion and prescription or weight-control diets actually stay separated. That selective access is the feature that turns chaos into a managed routine, and it is why this feeder anchors the multi-pet tier. For households juggling different diets or a bully at mealtime, this is the practical fix.

en.commentary: My order holds and the Petlibro Granary Camera stays my number one. It is the complete package at its price, pairing accurate portion control and a robust anti-jam mechanism with a 1080p camera, 145 degree wide view, and IR night vision so you can watch and feed from your phone. A quadruple-sealed hopper keeps kibble fresh and it handles up to ten meals a day, and dual-band Wi-Fi means it holds a stable connection on a crowded home network. Reviewers this cycle keep calling it the best they have tested, and I agree. The Petkit YumShare Dual-Hopper 2 stays second as the multi-pet answer, since two hoppers let you mix food types or portion two animals separately, though you pay a premium for it. Petlibro's own lineup fills much of my board because it covers the real use cases well, the Polar for wet food, the One RFID for gating a specific pet, each earning its rank. The steady read stays the same, a feeder lives or dies on dispensing reliability first, then camera and app, so I weight it that way. Buy the Granary for most single and two-cat homes, step up to the Dual-Hopper only if you truly need separate food streams.

en.commentary: The big story this week is the Petlibro Granary 2 series announced June 18, with AI pet recognition, gram-level weight tracking, and a smart refill mode. Pre-orders are open and units ship July 27, so I am holding my current order steady until I put a shipping unit on my own counter and watch it dispense for a full week. Announcements do not earn rank points here, real dispensing does. That is exactly why the Granary Camera keeps the top spot: it pairs a genuinely useful monitoring camera with the dependable auger that made this line my default recommendation for single-cat and easy multi-cat homes. The PetKit YumShare Dual Hopper 2 stays right behind it for anyone feeding two flavors or a picky pair, since the dual bins and the crisp 1080p feed remain the most complete premium package. I still steer wet-food households to the Polar, its rotating cold storage handles pate cleanly and the price feels fair for what you get. For pure mechanical peace of mind, the HomeRun PF20 keeps its high reliability score because it just keeps feeding through power blips. SureFeed remains my pick for the microchip crowd protecting a prescription diet. My advice this week stays the same: buy the proven feeder your pet needs today, and let the Granary 2 prove itself in real kitchens first.

en.commentary: The two brands that matter here keep pulling ahead, and the ranking holds. The Petlibro Granary Camera stays my number one because it nails the things that actually keep a pet fed: reliable dispensing, no mandatory subscription to use the camera or app, and portion control reviewers consistently trust. It carries one of the largest review bases of any feeder on Amazon, over 11,000 ratings, which tells me it has survived a lot of real homes and held up, and its price sits in a genuinely reasonable window. That track record is why it leads on value. The Petkit YumShare Dual-Hopper 2 stays second, and if you feed two different diets it is the one I hand you, with its dual hoppers, sharp AI motion-clip camera, and a longer warranty than most rivals. The Petlibro Polar Wet Food holds third as the specialist for pets that need refrigerated wet meals, a niche nobody else covers as cleanly. Further down, the Petlibro One RFID and SureFeed keep their spots as the multi-pet gatekeepers that only open for the right collar or chip. I held every score this week. The summer coverage reinforced Petlibro's track record and Petkit's price-and-warranty edge, both already on the board, so no rank changes.

zh-tw.commentary: 這裡真正重要的兩個品牌持續往前拉，排名維持。Petlibro Granary Camera 穩坐我的第一，因為它把真正能讓寵物吃飽的事情做對了：出料可靠、用相機跟 App 不用強制訂閱、分量控制被評測者一致信任。它在 Amazon 上有任何餵食器裡數一數二大的評論基數，超過 11,000 則評分，這告訴我它撐過了很多真實家庭還是好好的，而且價格落在真的合理的區間。這個口碑就是它性價比領先的原因。Petkit YumShare Dual-Hopper 2 守住第二，如果你要餵兩種不同的飲食，我會把這台交給你，它有雙料倉、清晰的 AI 動態剪輯相機，保固還比多數對手長。Petlibro Polar Wet Food 守住第三，是需要冷藏濕食的寵物的專門解，這個利基沒有別人做得這麼乾淨。再往下，Petlibro One RFID 跟 SureFeed 保住位置，當多寵物家庭的守門員，只對正確的項圈或晶片打開。這週我把每個分數都維持住。夏天的報導強化的是 Petlibro 的口碑跟 Petkit 的價格保固優勢，這兩點榜單本來就有，所以排名沒變。
